items.title: SoftBank Plans €75 Billion Investment in French AI Data Centers
items.summary: SoftBank announced a massive €75 billion investment to construct new data centers in France, signaling a major commitment to AI infrastructure in Europe.

items.title: GitHub Copilot's New Token-Based Billing Sparks Developer Outcry
items.summary: GitHub Copilot's shift to a token-based billing model has caused significant frustration and consternation among developers due to unexpected cost increases.

items.title: Meta Reportedly Developing an AI Pendant for Wearable Computing
items.summary: Meta is reportedly working on an AI-powered pendant, indicating a potential new direction for wearable AI devices and human-computer interaction.

items.title: Nous Research Launches NousCoder-14B, a Free Open-Source Alternative to Claude Code
items.summary: Nous Research has released NousCoder-14B, an open-source coding model, positioning it as a free and competitive alternative to expensive commercial tools like Claude Code.

items.title: Why Far Looks Up: Probing Spatial Representation in Vision-Language Models
items.summary: New research delves into how vision-language models represent spatial relationships, offering insights into their understanding of object positions and interactions.

items.title: Listen Labs Raises $69M to Scale AI Customer Interviews After Viral Stunt
items.summary: Listen Labs secured $69 million in funding to expand its AI-driven customer interview platform, following a successful viral billboard hiring campaign.

items.title: UniSteer: Text-Guided Flow Matching for Versatile LLM Steering
items.summary: Researchers introduce UniSteer, a novel method using text-guided flow matching in activation space to enable more versatile and precise steering of large language models.

items.title: Hermes Agent's Increasing Capabilities Come with a Rising Cost
items.summary: As the Hermes AI agent becomes more intelligent and capable, developers are noting a significant increase in its operational costs, raising concerns about long-term affordability.

items.title: Google Highlights 12 Major Moments from I/O 2026 Keynote
items.summary: Google's AI Blog recaps 12 significant announcements and developments from the I/O 2026 keynote, emphasizing advancements in AI and technology.

items.title: LoMo: Local Modality Substitution for Deeper Vision-Language Fusion
items.summary: A new paper proposes LoMo, a technique for local modality substitution that aims to achieve deeper and more effective fusion between vision and language models.

items.title: OpenAI Shares Playbook for Trustworthy Third-Party AI Evaluations
items.summary: OpenAI has published a shared playbook outlining best practices for conducting trustworthy third-party evaluations of AI systems, focusing on safety and reliability.

items.title: Your AI Agent Should Initiate Communication First
items.summary: An article argues that AI agents should be proactive in initiating communication with users, rather than solely reacting to prompts, to enhance user experience and utility.

items.title: Skill0.5: Joint Skill Internalization and Utilization for OOD Generalization in Agentic RL
items.summary: New research introduces Skill0.5, a method for agentic reinforcement learning that combines skill internalization and utilization to improve generalization in out-of-distribution scenarios.

items.title: When Cloud Agents Meet Device Agents: Lessons from Hybrid Multi-Agent Systems
items.summary: A paper explores the challenges and insights gained from integrating cloud-based AI agents with device-side agents in hybrid multi-agent systems.

items.title: Inference Theft Is the New AI App Security Bug: Protecting LLM Endpoints
items.summary: An article highlights 'inference theft' as a critical new security vulnerability for AI applications and provides guidance on how to protect large language model endpoints.
